rationale: Business & Management is the level-1 I added against majority vote because its top journals (AMJ, SMJ, MS, MISQ) publish a distinct intellectual community that doesn't show up in Economics. Twelve subspecialties match the canonical business-school department structure (marketing, OB, strategy, operations, accounting, MIS) plus modern emergent areas (sustainability, entrepreneurship). I kept it small to respect the actual paper volume — Business & Management is only 2% of all papers, so a wide level-2 would feel padded.

subspecialties.name: Strategy & Organization
subspecialties.tagline: Competitive strategy, M&A, alliances, firm capabilities.
covers: competitive strategy, dynamic capabilities, M&A, alliances and networks, strategic decision making, corporate governance strategy
SMJ, AMJ strategy, ASQ

subspecialties.name: Organizational Behavior
subspecialties.tagline: Leadership, teams, motivation, culture, well-being at work.
covers: leadership research, teams, motivation, organizational culture, well-being at work, organizational identity, diversity in organizations
AMJ OB, JAP, OS, OBHDP
explicitly_excludes: I-O psychology methods → Brain & Cognitive Sciences

subspecialties.name: Human Resource Management
covers: HRM research, recruitment, compensation systems, performance management, talent management, employment relations from HR angle
HRM, HRMJ, J Vocat Behav

subspecialties.name: Marketing
subspecialties.tagline: Consumer behavior, branding, pricing, digital marketing, CX.
covers: consumer behavior, branding, pricing strategy, marketing analytics, digital marketing, customer experience, advertising research
JM, JMR, JCR, Mark Sci
explicitly_excludes: consumer psychology → Brain & Cognitive Sciences (Social Psych)

subspecialties.name: Operations & Supply Chain Management
subspecialties.tagline: Supply chain, inventory, scheduling, service operations.
covers: supply chain management, inventory management, service operations, scheduling research, lean and Six Sigma, sustainable operations
Mfg Serv Oper Manag, JOM, POM

subspecialties.name: Accounting & Auditing
subspecialties.tagline: Financial accounting, managerial, audit, taxation research.
covers: financial accounting research, managerial accounting, audit research, tax research, accounting information systems, ESG reporting
JAR, JAE, TAR, RAST
explicitly_excludes: tax policy → Public Economics under Economics & Finance

subspecialties.name: Information Systems & Digital Innovation
subspecialties.tagline: IS strategy, digital platforms, IT productivity, AI adoption.
covers: information systems research, digital platforms management, IT productivity, AI adoption in firms, digital transformation, business analytics
MISQ, ISR, JMIS

subspecialties.name: Entrepreneurship & Innovation Management
subspecialties.tagline: New ventures, venture capital, R&D management, startup ecosystems.
covers: new venture creation, venture capital research, R&D management, innovation systems, technology transfer, intrapreneurship, startup ecosystems
JBV, Entrep Theory Pract, Res Policy mgmt

subspecialties.name: International Business
subspecialties.tagline: MNEs, FDI, cross-cultural management, emerging markets.
covers: multinational enterprise research, FDI strategy, cross-cultural management, emerging market business, global supply chains from business angle, internationalization
JIBS, Global Strategy J

subspecialties.name: Corporate Finance & Management
subspecialties.tagline: Corporate governance, M&A from finance, executive compensation.
covers: corporate governance research, M&A from finance-mgmt angle, executive compensation research, capital structure, IPO research
JFE corporate, JCF, JFM

subspecialties.name: Business Ethics & Sustainability
subspecialties.tagline: CSR, ESG, business ethics, stakeholder theory, sustainability mgmt.
covers: business ethics research, CSR, ESG management, stakeholder theory, sustainability management, social entrepreneurship
J Bus Ethics, BSE, AMR ethics
explicitly_excludes: environmental policy → Public Health / Social Sciences

subspecialties.name: Public Management & Nonprofit Management
subspecialties.tagline: Public administration research, nonprofit governance, NGO management.
covers: public administration research, nonprofit governance, NGO management, public service motivation, government performance research
JPART, PAR, Nonprofit Volunt Sect Q

uncovered_concerns: Considered Real Estate as its own card — folds under Finance (Corporate Finance & Mgmt) or Public Management (urban planning).
Considered Tourism & Hospitality Management — folds under Marketing or Operations.
Considered Family Business research — small; folds under Entrepreneurship.
